title: Creating Strong Passwords
drDigitalIntro: A strong password is your first line of defense online. A good password is long (at least 12 characters), uses a mix of uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols. Avoid using your name, birthday, or common words. Each account should have a different password — if one site gets hacked, your other accounts stay safe. Let's practice making a strong password!

steps.say: Go to the Password Tester tab.
Type a weak password like 'password123' and see the strength meter.
Check the strength — it's weak. Click Check Strength.
Now type a strong password with letters, numbers, and symbols.
Check the strength again — see the difference!

drDigitalSuccess: You tested password strength! Long passwords with mixed characters are much harder to guess.
drDigitalHint: Type in the password field, then click Check Strength to see how secure it is.
